frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: canonical neural computation rescaling choice values by context

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: DN as efficient encoder of Pareto-distributed stimuli

frameworks.name: environment-matched encoding
frameworks.one_line: subjects recalibrate DN parameters to fit each environment's statistics

core_question: Does the brain switch encoding functions across choice environments, or does divisive normalization persist outside Pareto-distributed contexts?
key_assumptions: choice values are encoded through a divisive computation
encoding can be calibrated to environmental stimulus statistics
within-subject design isolates environment effects on choice
manipulations: choice-set value distribution: Pareto vs uniform
within-subject exposure to two distinct environments
key_findings: subjects' choices are better described by divisive coding in both environments
DN function is recalibrated to match each environment's statistical properties
interpretations: divisive normalization generalizes beyond Pareto stimuli to other choice environments
the brain adapts DN parameters rather than swapping encoding strategies
